name: ExtensionDeviceDispense_Receiver
title: R5: Who collected the device or where the medication was delivered (new)
purpose:
This extension is part of the cross-version definitions generated to enable use of the
element `DeviceDispense.receiver` as defined in FHIR R5
in FHIR R4.

The source element is defined as:
`DeviceDispense.receiver` 0..1 `Reference(http://hl7.org/fhir/StructureDefinition/Patient,http://hl7.org/fhir/StructureDefinition/Practitioner,http://hl7.org/fhir/StructureDefinition/RelatedPerson,http://hl7.org/fhir/StructureDefinition/Location,http://hl7.org/fhir/StructureDefinition/PractitionerRole)`

Across FHIR versions, the element set has been mapped as:
* R5 `DeviceDispense.receiver` 0..1 `Reference(http://hl7.org/fhir/StructureDefinition/Patient,http://hl7.org/fhir/StructureDefinition/Practitioner,http://hl7.org/fhir/StructureDefinition/RelatedPerson,http://hl7.org/fhir/StructureDefinition/Location,http://hl7.org/fhir/StructureDefinition/PractitionerRole)`

Following are the generation technical comments:
Element `DeviceDispense.receiver` is not mapped to FHIR R4, since FHIR R5 `DeviceDispense` is not mapped.
Element `DeviceDispense.receiver` has no mapping targets in FHIR R4. Typically, this is because the element has been added (is a new element).

element.short: R5: Who collected the device or where the medication was delivered (new)
element.comment:
Element `DeviceDispense.receiver` is not mapped to FHIR R4, since FHIR R5 `DeviceDispense` is not mapped.
Element `DeviceDispense.receiver` has no mapping targets in FHIR R4. Typically, this is because the element has been added (is a new element).

element.short: Who collected the device or where the medication was delivered
element.definition: Identifies the person who picked up the device or the person or location where the device was delivered.  This may be a patient or their caregiver, but some cases exist where it can be a healthcare professional or a location.
